recipe.name: Korean Beef + Broccoli Bowls

ingredient_groups.heading: Beef
items: 2 lb ground beef (93% lean works great)
1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated (or 1 tsp ground)

ingredient_groups.heading: Sauce (whisk in a jar/bowl)
items: 2 tbsp rice vinegar (or apple cider in a pinch)
1 tsp red pepper flakes (1/2 tsp for kid-friendly)

ingredient_groups.heading: Broccoli
items: 2 large heads broccoli, cut into bite-size florets (~6 cups)

ingredient_groups.heading: Serve with
items: 3 cups jasmine rice, cooked (~1.5 cups dry)
Optional: kimchi or quick-pickled carrots for crunch

all_ingredients: 2 lb ground beef (93% lean works great)
1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated (or 1 tsp ground)
2 tbsp rice vinegar (or apple cider in a pinch)
1 tsp red pepper flakes (1/2 tsp for kid-friendly)
2 large heads broccoli, cut into bite-size florets (~6 cups)
3 cups jasmine rice, cooked (~1.5 cups dry)
Optional: kimchi or quick-pickled carrots for crunch
steps: Start rice first (longest part).

**Broccoli, separate pan.** Big skillet, medium-high, neutral oil hot. Broccoli in single layer, don't crowd. Sit 2 min for char. Toss, another 2 min. Splash 2 tbsp water, lid on 90 sec. Lid off, salt. Should be bright green with brown edges, still has snap. Pull off heat.

**Beef, separate skillet.** Sesame oil, medium-high. Brown beef hard, break it small, 6-8 min. Drop heat to medium. Push beef aside, garlic + ginger in empty space, 30 sec until fragrant. Stir together.
Pour sauce in, stir. Thickens in ~90 sec from cornstarch. If too thick, splash of water.
Taste. Flat? More soy. Too salty? Pinch more brown sugar.
Dump broccoli into beef pan at the end, toss 30 sec to coat in sauce.
recipe.notes:
Morning Prep (optional, saves 15 min in evening)

  1. Whisk full sauce in a jar, refrigerate. Shake before using.
  2. Mince garlic + ginger together, small container, fridge.
  3. Cut broccoli into florets, gallon ziploc, fridge crisper.
  4. Measure dry rice + water into rice cooker pot, leave on counter. Don't pre-cook the beef or broccoli — reheating ruins both.

Assembly

Rice in bowl. Beef + broccoli on top. Sesame seeds over the top. Sriracha for anyone over 12.

Notes

  • Two pans for broccoli + beef is non-negotiable. Cooking broccoli in beef pan = soggy gray broccoli from sauce moisture.
  • 93% lean is fine here — sauce carries the richness.
  • Leftovers: cold beef on rice next day is unreasonably good. Or fry an egg on top.
  • Sub English cucumber sliced for cold crunch contrast if desired.

Kid Reactions (expected)

  • Thomas: deconstructed, beef and rice not touching, no green stuff visible.
  • William: will pick around broccoli; deal = 3 pieces minimum.
  • Henry + Charlie: inhale and ask for seconds.
  • Nancy: yes. No tzatziki.
recipe.body_html:
Korean Beef + Broccoli Bowls

Serves: 6 Active time: 25 min Macros (Mike's portion): ~600 cal / 60g protein (est. with 93/7 beef)

Ingredients

Beef

  • 2 lb ground beef (93% lean works great)
  • 1 tbsp toasted sesame oil (for cooking)
  • 6 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated (or 1 tsp ground)

Sauce (whisk in a jar/bowl)

  • 1/2 cup soy sauce
  • 1/3 cup brown sugar, packed
  • 2 tbsp rice vinegar (or apple cider in a pinch)
  • 1 tbsp toasted sesame oil
  • 1 tsp red pepper flakes (1/2 tsp for kid-friendly)
  • 1 tsp black pepper
  • 1 tbsp cornstarch

Broccoli

  • 2 large heads broccoli, cut into bite-size florets (~6 cups)
  • 1 tbsp neutral oil (avocado or vegetable)
  • Pinch of salt
  • 2 tbsp water

Serve with

  • 3 cups jasmine rice, cooked (~1.5 cups dry)
  • Toasted sesame seeds
  • Sriracha on the table
  • Optional: kimchi or quick-pickled carrots for crunch

Morning Prep (optional, saves 15 min in evening)

  1. Whisk full sauce in a jar, refrigerate. Shake before using.
  2. Mince garlic + ginger together, small container, fridge.
  3. Cut broccoli into florets, gallon ziploc, fridge crisper.
  4. Measure dry rice + water into rice cooker pot, leave on counter.

Don't pre-cook the beef or broccoli — reheating ruins both.

Method

  1. Start rice first (longest part).
  2. Broccoli, separate pan. Big skillet, medium-high, neutral oil hot. Broccoli in single layer, don't crowd. Sit 2 min for char. Toss, another 2 min. Splash 2 tbsp water, lid on 90 sec. Lid off, salt. Should be bright green with brown edges, still has snap. Pull off heat.
  3. Beef, separate skillet. Sesame oil, medium-high. Brown beef hard, break it small, 6-8 min. Drop heat to medium. Push beef aside, garlic + ginger in empty space, 30 sec until fragrant. Stir together.
  4. Pour sauce in, stir. Thickens in ~90 sec from cornstarch. If too thick, splash of water.
  5. Taste. Flat? More soy. Too salty? Pinch more brown sugar.
  6. Dump broccoli into beef pan at the end, toss 30 sec to coat in sauce.

Assembly

Rice in bowl. Beef + broccoli on top. Sesame seeds over the top. Sriracha for anyone over 12.

Notes

  • Two pans for broccoli + beef is non-negotiable. Cooking broccoli in beef pan = soggy gray broccoli from sauce moisture.
  • 93% lean is fine here — sauce carries the richness.
  • Leftovers: cold beef on rice next day is unreasonably good. Or fry an egg on top.
  • Sub English cucumber sliced for cold crunch contrast if desired.

Kid Reactions (expected)

  • Thomas: deconstructed, beef and rice not touching, no green stuff visible.
  • William: will pick around broccoli; deal = 3 pieces minimum.
  • Henry + Charlie: inhale and ask for seconds.
  • Nancy: yes. No tzatziki.

recipes.name: Chicken Fajitas (Cast Iron)

items: 1.5–2 lb chicken breasts, sliced into 1/4" strips
2 medium zucchini, halved lengthwise then sliced 1/4" thick
1.5 cups corn (frozen or fresh kernels off the cob)

all_ingredients: 1.5–2 lb chicken breasts, sliced into 1/4" strips
2 medium zucchini, halved lengthwise then sliced 1/4" thick
1.5 cups corn (frozen or fresh kernels off the cob)
steps: **Marinate:** Toss sliced chicken with 1 tbsp olive oil and 1.5 tbsp fajita seasoning. Let it sit 15+ min while you prep veg.
**Heat the cast iron:** Crank a 12" cast iron over medium-high until it's smoking lightly (~3 min).
**Sear chicken:** Add chicken in a single layer, don't crowd. Cook untouched 2–3 min until charred, flip and cook another 2 min. Pull to a plate. Work in two batches if needed.
**Cook onions:** Add remaining 1 tbsp oil and onions to the same pan. 3–4 min until they get color.

**Add zucchini + corn:** Toss in zucchini and corn with the rest of the fajita seasoning + pinch of salt. Cook 4–5 min, stirring once or twice. You want char on the zucchini, not mush. Don't stir constantly.
**Combine:** Return chicken (and any juices) to the pan, toss everything together 1 min to reheat.
**Warm tortillas:** Wrap in foil and stick in a 300°F oven for 5 min, or char them one at a time over an open gas flame for 10 sec/side.
**Serve:** Pile into tortillas. Toppings optional but lime juice, cilantro, salsa, or a dab of sour cream all work.
recipes.notes:
Pitfalls

  • Don't crowd the pan or chicken steams instead of searing.
  • Zucchini turns to mush fast — keep it moving and pull it before it looks done.
  • Cast iron stays scorching after you turn it off, so move stuff off the heat once chicken is back in.
recipes.body_html:
Chicken Fajitas (Cast Iron, with Zucchini + Corn)

Serves 4–5. ~600 cal / 48–62g protein per serving (2 tortillas + filling).

Ingredients

  • 1.5–2 lb chicken breasts, sliced into 1/4" strips
  • 2 tbsp fajita seasoning (or to taste)
  • 2 medium onions, sliced into half-moons
  • 2 medium zucchini, halved lengthwise then sliced 1/4" thick
  • 1.5 cups corn (frozen or fresh kernels off the cob)
  • 2 tbsp olive oil, divided
  • 8–10 tortillas
  • Salt + pepper

Steps

  1. Marinate: Toss sliced chicken with 1 tbsp olive oil and 1.5 tbsp fajita seasoning. Let it sit 15+ min while you prep veg.
  2. Heat the cast iron: Crank a 12" cast iron over medium-high until it's smoking lightly (~3 min).
  3. Sear chicken: Add chicken in a single layer, don't crowd. Cook untouched 2–3 min until charred, flip and cook another 2 min. Pull to a plate. Work in two batches if needed.
  4. Cook onions: Add remaining 1 tbsp oil and onions to the same pan. 3–4 min until they get color.
  5. Add zucchini + corn: Toss in zucchini and corn with the rest of the fajita seasoning + pinch of salt. Cook 4–5 min, stirring once or twice. You want char on the zucchini, not mush. Don't stir constantly.
  6. Combine: Return chicken (and any juices) to the pan, toss everything together 1 min to reheat.
  7. Warm tortillas: Wrap in foil and stick in a 300°F oven for 5 min, or char them one at a time over an open gas flame for 10 sec/side.
  8. Serve: Pile into tortillas. Toppings optional but lime juice, cilantro, salsa, or a dab of sour cream all work.

Pitfalls

  • Don't crowd the pan or chicken steams instead of searing.
  • Zucchini turns to mush fast — keep it moving and pull it before it looks done.
  • Cast iron stays scorching after you turn it off, so move stuff off the heat once chicken is back in.

recipes.name: Italian sausage + onion + zucchini

items: Yellow onions (2), cut into thick wedges

all_ingredients: Yellow onions (2), cut into thick wedges
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F. Start rice on the stove or in a rice cooker.
On a large sheet pan, toss zucchini, onion wedges, and cherry tomatoes with olive oil, Italian seasoning, salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Nestle whole sausage links among the veg. Don't crowd — use two pans if needed.
Roast 25-30 min, flipping sausages once at the halfway point. Sausages should be browned and internal temp 160°F. Tomatoes should be blistered.
Slice sausages on the bias. Serve over rice with pan juices spooned on top. Parm to finish if you've got it.
recipes.notes:
Notes

  • If sausages are pre-cooked (smoked/kielbasa-style), drop oven time to 20 min total.
  • Sub baby bell peppers or quartered fennel for the tomatoes if needed.
  • Leftovers reheat well; chop everything and toss with pasta the next day.
recipes.body_html:
Italian sausage + onion + zucchini

Sheet pan. One pan, zero brain cells. Serves 6.

Ingredients

  • Italian sausage links (~2 lb)
  • Zucchini (3-4 medium), cut into 1/2-inch half-moons
  • Yellow onions (2), cut into thick wedges
  • Cherry tomatoes (1 pint), whole
  • Rice (jasmine or long-grain), 2 cups dry
  • Olive oil (3-4 tbsp)
  • Italian seasoning (2 tsp)
  • Salt + pepper
  • Garlic powder (optional, 1 tsp)
  • Parmesan to finish (optional)

Steps

  1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Start rice on the stove or in a rice cooker.
  2. On a large sheet pan, toss zucchini, onion wedges, and cherry tomatoes with olive oil, Italian seasoning, salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
  3. Nestle whole sausage links among the veg. Don't crowd — use two pans if needed.
  4. Roast 25-30 min, flipping sausages once at the halfway point. Sausages should be browned and internal temp 160°F. Tomatoes should be blistered.
  5. Slice sausages on the bias. Serve over rice with pan juices spooned on top. Parm to finish if you've got it.

Notes

  • If sausages are pre-cooked (smoked/kielbasa-style), drop oven time to 20 min total.
  • Sub baby bell peppers or quartered fennel for the tomatoes if needed.
  • Leftovers reheat well; chop everything and toss with pasta the next day.

Korean Beef + Broccoli Bowls

Ingredients

  • 2 lb ground beef (93% lean works great)

Sauce (whisk in a jar/bowl)

items: condensed cream of chicken with herbs soup
1 cup shredded cheddar cheese (layer under Ritz topping)

all_ingredients: condensed cream of chicken with herbs soup
1 cup shredded cheddar cheese (layer under Ritz topping)
recipes.body_html:
Ritz Chicken Casserole

Family favorite. Use store-bought rotisserie chicken. Last made 11/11/22.

Ingredients

  • condensed cream of chicken with herbs soup
  • sour cream
  • green onion
  • rotisserie chicken
  • Ritz crackers
  • salted butter
  • 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese (layer under Ritz topping)

Steps

350F, 25-30 min. Mix soup + sour cream, stir in chicken + broccoli, spread in 9x9. Top with shredded cheddar, then crushed Ritz mixed with melted butter. Bake until golden.

recipes.name: Tuscan Chicken Pasta

ingredient_groups.heading: Pasta + Sauce
items: ¼ cup sun-dried tomatoes, softened in hot water and chopped
1 cup parmesan cheese, grated (plus more for garnish)

all_ingredients: ¼ cup sun-dried tomatoes, softened in hot water and chopped
1 cup parmesan cheese, grated (plus more for garnish)
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F. Drizzle 2 tbsp olive oil over the chicken on all sides, rub it in.
Combine paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Sprinkle over chicken on all sides.
In a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high, heat the remaining 1 tbsp oil. Sear chicken 3 min per side until browned (not cooked through).
Transfer skillet to oven, cook 10–15 min until internal temp hits 165°F. Move chicken to a plate, tent to keep warm. ### Pasta + Sauce
Boil rigatoni in salted water, cook al dente (~14 min). Drain.
In the same skillet, melt butter over medium. Add shallots + sun-dried tomatoes, cook 3–5 min until softened. Add garlic, cook 1 more min.
Add tomato paste, oregano, paprika, garlic powder, salt, pepper. Stir.
Add heavy cream and milk, stir to combine.
Add spinach, parmesan, and cherry tomatoes. Reduce heat to low, simmer 8–10 min until cheese melts and spinach wilts.
Add cooked pasta, toss to coat. Slice or cube the chicken and fold in.
Serve topped with extra parmesan and parsley. Salt + pepper to taste.
recipes.notes:
Pitfalls

  • Chicken won't be cooked through after searing — the oven finishes it. Pull at 165°F so it doesn't dry out.
  • Soften the sun-dried tomatoes in hot water first or they stay leathery.
  • Sauce thickens as it sits, so don't over-reduce before adding pasta.
  • Nancy-safe: no mushrooms, peppers, fish, feta, lemon, or cilantro. Clear.
recipes.body_html:
Tuscan Chicken Pasta

Rigatoni in a creamy parmesan-tomato sauce with spinach and sun-dried tomatoes, topped with baked seasoned chicken. Serves 6. ~471 cal / ~30g protein per serving. Weigh your portion and load extra chicken + parmesan to hit your macro target.

Ingredients

Chicken

  • 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts (~1 lb)
  • 3 tbsp olive oil, divided
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1 tsp kosher salt
  • ½ tsp black pepper

Pasta + Sauce

  • 8 oz rigatoni, cooked al dente
  • 4 tbsp (½ stick) unsalted butter
  • ¼ cup shallot, finely diced
  • ¼ cup sun-dried tomatoes, softened in hot water and chopped
  • 1 tsp garlic, minced
  • 2 tbsp tomato paste
  • 1 tsp oregano
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • ½ tsp garlic powder
  • ½ tsp kosher salt
  • ¼ tsp black pepper
  • 1½ cups heavy cream
  • 1½ cups milk
  • 1 cup fresh spinach
  • 1 cup parmesan cheese, grated (plus more for garnish)
  • 1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
  • Fresh parsley, for garnish
  • Salt + pepper, to taste

Steps

Chicken

  1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Drizzle 2 tbsp olive oil over the chicken on all sides, rub it in.
  2. Combine paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Sprinkle over chicken on all sides.
  3. In a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high, heat the remaining 1 tbsp oil. Sear chicken 3 min per side until browned (not cooked through).
  4. Transfer skillet to oven, cook 10–15 min until internal temp hits 165°F. Move chicken to a plate, tent to keep warm.

Pasta + Sauce

  1. Boil rigatoni in salted water, cook al dente (~14 min). Drain.
  2. In the same skillet, melt butter over medium. Add shallots + sun-dried tomatoes, cook 3–5 min until softened. Add garlic, cook 1 more min.
  3. Add tomato paste, oregano, paprika, garlic powder, salt, pepper. Stir.
  4. Add heavy cream and milk, stir to combine.
  5. Add spinach, parmesan, and cherry tomatoes. Reduce heat to low, simmer 8–10 min until cheese melts and spinach wilts.
  6. Add cooked pasta, toss to coat. Slice or cube the chicken and fold in.
  7. Serve topped with extra parmesan and parsley. Salt + pepper to taste.

Pitfalls

  • Chicken won't be cooked through after searing — the oven finishes it. Pull at 165°F so it doesn't dry out.
  • Soften the sun-dried tomatoes in hot water first or they stay leathery.
  • Sauce thickens as it sits, so don't over-reduce before adding pasta.
  • Nancy-safe: no mushrooms, peppers, fish, feta, lemon, or cilantro. Clear.

recipes.name: White Chicken Chili
steps: Drain and rinse black beans.
Place chicken at the bottom of the crock pot, and pour the whole can of corn, rotel and rinsed/drained black beans.
Top with seasonings and ranch mix. Stir together.
Place cream cheese on top.
Cover with lid and cook on low for 6-8 hours.
Stir cream cheese into chili. Use 2 forks to shred chicken. Stir together and serve.
recipes.body_html:
White Chicken Chili

Ingredients

  • 1 can black beans
  • 1 can corn
  • 1 can rotel
  • 2 chicken breasts
  • 1 pkg ranch dressing mix
  • 1 cream cheese
  • 1 pound mexican cheese
  • 1 bag tortilla chips
  • 1 pkg sour cream

Steps

  1. Drain and rinse black beans.
  2. Place chicken at the bottom of the crock pot, and pour the whole can of corn, rotel and rinsed/drained black beans.
  3. Top with seasonings and ranch mix. Stir together.
  4. Place cream cheese on top.
  5. Cover with lid and cook on low for 6-8 hours.
  6. Stir cream cheese into chili. Use 2 forks to shred chicken. Stir together and serve.

Toppings

  • Mexican cheese (shredded)
  • Tortilla chips
  • Sour cream

recipes.name: Copycat Honey Baked Ham

items: 8 lb bone-in spiral sliced half ham

all_ingredients: 8 lb bone-in spiral sliced half ham
steps: Trim ham to fit slow cooker if needed. Pat dry with paper towels (the bag liquid will make the butter slide right off otherwise).
Combine melted butter + honey. Massage over ham, getting some between the slices.
Cover and cook on **LOW for 4-5 hours**.
Preheat broiler to HIGH. Line a roasting pan with heavy-duty foil.
Combine sugar, seasoned salt, onion powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, ginger, cloves, paprika, allspice in a small bowl.
Transfer ham to roasting pan. Pat **half** of the sugar mixture over the top.
Broil a few minutes until bubbly and caramelized. Remove from oven.
In a small saucepan, add the **remaining** sugar mixture + 3 Tbsp water OR ham juices from the slow cooker (bourbon swap works too). Stir, bring to a boil, boil ~1 min, remove from heat.
Brush/pour glaze over the ham. Broil **1-2 more minutes** — watch it, sugar burns fast.
Rest 5-10 min at room temp so the glaze hardens into the crackly crust.
recipes.notes:
Notes / pitfalls

  • DRY the ham before the butter/honey rub or it slides off.
  • Two broil passes, not one. First pass sets the crust, second pass glazes it.
  • Don't walk away during broiling. Carbonized sugar = sad ham.
  • Bourbon in the saucepan instead of plain water adds depth.
recipes.body_html:
Copycat Honey Baked Ham

Slow-cooker spiral ham with the iconic broiled-sugar crust. Cheaper than the $75 Honey Baked store version.

Prep ~20 min, cook ~4-5 hours in slow cooker. Serves ~10.

Ingredients

  • 8 lb bone-in spiral sliced half ham
  • 2 Tbsp butter, melted
  • 3 Tbsp honey

Glaze (dry rub)

  • 1½ cups granulated sugar
  • ½ tsp seasoned salt
  • ½ tsp onion powder
  • ½ tsp ground cinnamon
  • ½ tsp ground nutmeg
  • ¼ tsp ground ginger
  • ¼ tsp ground cloves
  • ¼ tsp paprika
  • pinch of allspice

Steps

  1. Trim ham to fit slow cooker if needed. Pat dry with paper towels (the bag liquid will make the butter slide right off otherwise).
  2. Combine melted butter + honey. Massage over ham, getting some between the slices.
  3. Cover and cook on LOW for 4-5 hours.
  4. Preheat broiler to HIGH. Line a roasting pan with heavy-duty foil.
  5. Combine sugar, seasoned salt, onion powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, ginger, cloves, paprika, allspice in a small bowl.
  6. Transfer ham to roasting pan. Pat half of the sugar mixture over the top.
  7. Broil a few minutes until bubbly and caramelized. Remove from oven.
  8. In a small saucepan, add the remaining sugar mixture + 3 Tbsp water OR ham juices from the slow cooker (bourbon swap works too). Stir, bring to a boil, boil ~1 min, remove from heat.
  9. Brush/pour glaze over the ham. Broil 1-2 more minutes — watch it, sugar burns fast.
  10. Rest 5-10 min at room temp so the glaze hardens into the crackly crust.

Notes / pitfalls

  • DRY the ham before the butter/honey rub or it slides off.
  • Two broil passes, not one. First pass sets the crust, second pass glazes it.
  • Don't walk away during broiling. Carbonized sugar = sad ham.
  • Bourbon in the saucepan instead of plain water adds depth.

recipes.name: Homemade Hamburger Helper
steps: Cook onion in olive oil.
Add garlic, cook a minute or so.
Add ground beef, brown.
Season with salt, pepper, paprika, garlic powder.
Stir in tomato paste + drained diced tomatoes.
Add beef broth + heavy cream.
Add uncooked noodles.
Cover, cook 15 min.
Uncover, cook 2 more min.
Off heat, top with cheddar, stir.
recipes.body_html:
Homemade Hamburger Helper

Nancy's. One-pot stovetop, beef + cheesy noodles.

Ingredients

  • 1 onion, diced
  • Olive oil
  • Garlic, minced
  • 1 package ground beef
  • Salt, pepper, paprika, garlic powder
  • 1 tbsp tomato paste
  • 1 can Italian-seasoned diced tomatoes, drained
  • 2 cups beef broth
  • 1 cup heavy cream
  • 2 cups uncooked noodles
  • Shredded cheddar

Steps

  1. Cook onion in olive oil.
  2. Add garlic, cook a minute or so.
  3. Add ground beef, brown.
  4. Season with salt, pepper, paprika, garlic powder.
  5. Stir in tomato paste + drained diced tomatoes.
  6. Add beef broth + heavy cream.
  7. Add uncooked noodles.
  8. Cover, cook 15 min.
  9. Uncover, cook 2 more min.
  10. Off heat, top with cheddar, stir.

Shopping list

  • Ground beef
  • Onion
  • Garlic
  • Beef broth
  • Heavy cream
  • Egg noodles (or similar)
  • Shredded cheddar
  • Tomato paste
  • Italian-seasoned diced tomatoes
